title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Boston duck boat overturned in Cambridge on a boat ramp after a tow rope snapped, injuring 11 people.
The incident occurred at 10 Museum Way near Education Street, with 31 people aboard; one person was seriously hurt.
Boston Duck Tours halted operations for the day and is collaborating with authorities to investigate the rollover.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins acquired winger JJ Peterka from Utah in exchange for two first-round picks, including Boston's 2026 and Florida's 2028.
Peterka, 24, has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three years with Buffalo and Utah, providing scoring depth for Boston.
General Manager Don Sweeney cited Peterka's age, skillset, and speed as key factors for the acquisition to bolster the Bruins' roster.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will cease covering GLP-1 and anti-obesity drugs solely for weight loss starting July 1.
The policy change affects 22,000 members and aims to save MassHealth $15 million annually, per Gov. Healey's budget.
Drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound will still be covered for conditions such as Type 2 diabetes, not for obesity alone.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins added seven players in Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft, focusing on goalies and skilled wingers.
Second-round pick Yuri Ivanov, a Russian goalie, posted a .924 save percentage with MHK Spartak Moskva in the MHL.
Cullen McCrate, a seventh-round pick, tallied 30 points with the USHL's Fargo Force, heading to Michigan State next season.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela resulted in at least 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing, per the Associated Press.
Red Sox players are honoring Venezuela with messages on their hats following the earthquakes.
David Ortiz raised funds at Fenway Park for Venezuela earthquake relief, encouraging donations through the Red Sox Foundation.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized an analytics report claiming he'd be the 7th-best player on an NBA team.
NBA insider Bobby Marks highlighted mixed opinions about Brown's game, sparking debate on analytics use in basketball.
Despite the report, Brown remains a valuable trade target, with significant interest from multiple NBA teams.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Yankees 4-1, marking their third consecutive win against the A.L.-leading team.
Jake Bennett pitched 6.1 innings for Boston, allowing just three hits and one run to earn his second win.
Masataka Yoshida and Anthony Seigler hit early home runs against Yankees pitcher Gerrit Cole, who struggled again with Boston.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade 2021 first-round pick Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan played 66 games with AHL's Colorado Eagles last season, scoring 26 points.
Lysell appeared in only 12 NHL games, recording three points, during his time with the Bruins.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium, showcasing a dynamic offensive style.
French forward Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick, with Michael Olise and Désiré Doué also contributing to the attack.
Despite defensive player rotations, Kylian Mbappe's assists highlighted France's depth and potential as tournament favorites.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich police are investigating a threat made by a middle school student against two classmates.
The threat was detected by GoGuardian, a safety app, on a school-issued device, prompting administrative alert.
The involved student is not attending school during the investigation, per Sandwich Public Schools officials.
